ORDER GRANTING IN PART AND DENYING IN PART DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING.JUDGE KAHN DISCLOSES THAT THE GENERAL COUNSEL OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA IS A CLOSE FRIEND OF HIS, BUT NEITHER THAT FRIENDSHIP NOR ANY OTHER MATTER CAUSES HIM TO BE ANYTHING OTHER THAN FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN DECIDING THIS CASE. DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST FOUR CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF DARA AUYEUNG IS OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF LABOR CODE SECTION 970, SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD, AND OVERRULED WITH AS TO THE THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND BREACH OF THE IMPLIED COVENANT. APPARENTLY MS. AUYEUNG HAS OR WILL DISMISS THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS) ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-13-2020 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-29-2019 Set For Trial On Mar-09-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-28-2019 Continued To Jun-12-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Maria Brown, esq. (415-545-8608) of Law Offices of Tanya Gomerman for Plaintiff.
Anna L. Padgett, esq. (415-369-3547) of Ogletree Deakins for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCSICO's DEMURRER TO Plaintiff's 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Judge Schulman discloses that he previously represented The Regents of the University of California. This will not affect Judge Schulman's ability to be fair and impartial in deciding this case.

The demurrer of defendant City and County of San Francisco (erroneously sued as Zuckerberg San Francisco General Hospital) is sustained with 20 days' leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to name the City as the proper party to this case rather than the hospital. (S.F. Charter Article 1, 1.101; Article IV, 4.110; S.F. Health Code, 111.)

The court rejects the City's remaining arguments. The City has failed to show that it is prohibited by statute or ordinance from entering into an enforceable employment agreement. (See Retired Employees Assoc. of Orange County v. County of Orange (2011) 52
Cal.4th 1171, 1176, 1182-1183.) The City Charter exempts from the civil service system certain "physicians and dentists serving in their professional capacity," and the City has not shown that a professional optometrist such as Plaintiff does not constitute a "physician" within the meaning of this provision. (Charter Article X, 10.104 (13); see Kreutzer v. City and County of San Francisco (2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 306, 309-310 [licensed physician who was board certified in psychiatry was hired by City into a position expressly classified by City Service Commission as exempt from civil service system].)

Plaintiff's allegations naming the Hospital (the City) as an employer are not a sham.
Plaintiff has consistently named the City as a party to the employment relationship since
filing the original complaint. At a minimum, the allegations present a factual issue regarding who the actual employer is and/or whether a joint employment relationship exists. Unlike Owens v. Kings Supermarket (1988) 198 Cal.App.3d 379, 384, plaintiff's allegations in this case are not contradictory; she has consistently alleged that the City was involved in her employment.

Lastly, plaintiff sufficiently alleges that the City failed to hire her because of her gender. The FEHA claim is thus well-stated.

Order is signed after hearing.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-28-2019 Continued To Apr-17-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCSICO's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Off Calendar per request of moving party. 2nd Amended complaint filed. (email 1-28-19)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Maria Bourn, Esq. 415-545-8608 of the Law Offices of Tanya Gomerman for Plaintiff.
Anna Padgett, Esq. 415-442-4810 of Ogletree Deakins for Defendant THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court informs parties of an additional line to the tentative ruling re: retired employees. The way the Court reads retired employees is that, contract of employment claims are permissible against a public entity as long as the public entity has the authority to contract and the nature of the claimed breach is not inconsistent with a Claifornia Statute. Defendant argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes)The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.Judge Kahn discloses that the General Counsel of the University of California is a close friend of his, but neither that friendship nor any other matter causes him to be anything other than fair and impartial in deciding this case. Defendant The Regents of the University of California's demurrer to the first four causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Dara Auyeung is off calendar as moot as to the first cause of action for violation of Labor Code section 970, sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the second cause of action for fraud, and overruled with as to the third and fourth causes of action for breach of contract and breach of the implied covenant. Apparently Ms. Auyeung has or will dismiss the first cause of action. The Regents have absolute immunity as to the fraud claim per Government Code section 818.8. Ms. Auyeung is given leave to name individual defendants on her fraud claim per Government Code section 822.2. A public entity can be liable on contractual employment claims. (Retired Employees Assoc. of Orange County v.County of Orange (2011) 52 Cal.4th 1171, 1182-83.) The Regents' effort in their reply memorandum to distinguish and limit Retired Employees is not supported by any language in Retired Employees or in any other case cited in the reply memorandum.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
